nodes.label: AI as enhanced search engine for relevant articles
nodes.grounding_span: I currently see LLM's as a type of search engine for finding articles that produce a higher rate of relevant articles compared to using Google Scholar

nodes.label: AI reliability in citations
nodes.grounding_span: the models will frequently find links that are not at all on topic, or have titles that sound like they could be relevant, but after reading, are not

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as overly critical and dismissive of early-stage ideas
nodes.grounding_span: I wish that it wouldn't decide to be completely dismissive of early-stage ideas, or express as much criticism about novel idea's

nodes.label: AI memory reliability in long conversations
nodes.label_negative: AI degradation in intelligence and focus over long conversations
nodes.grounding_span: it is noticeably dumber and less focused the more prompts I use

nodes.label: AI as efficient quiz/test creator
nodes.label_negative: AI wordiness and failure to distill complex ideas
nodes.grounding_span: LLMs quickly become wordy or fail to distill complex ideas into short questions

nodes.label: Research efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: it could provide links based on entire paragraphs describing my hypothesis or research ideas, instead of relying on keywords

nodes.label: Epistemic accuracy and understanding
nodes.grounding_span: I will independently verify the source, such as its authors or if it was done at a university

nodes.label: Creative freedom and exploration
nodes.grounding_span: the questions being asked in my research are harmless and more speculative than factual

nodes.label: Strong preference for AI search over Google
nodes.grounding_span: I immediately adopted it because it could provide links based on entire paragraphs

nodes.label: Frustration with AI errors and broken links
nodes.grounding_span: the models will frequently find links that are not at all on topic

nodes.label: Dismissive of AI as threat to specialized work
nodes.grounding_span: I wish that it wouldn't decide to be completely dismissive of early-stage ideas

nodes.label: Frustration with AI quality degradation over conversation length
nodes.grounding_span: it is noticeably dumber and less focused the more prompts I use

nodes.label: Reluctance to use AI for writing
nodes.grounding_span: LLMs quickly become wordy or fail to distill complex ideas into short questions

nodes.label: Systematic prompt engineering with chain-of-thought and verification steps
nodes.grounding_span: I frequently use chain of thought prompts that I come up with before starting a conversation, such as follow-up questions or asking the model to rank the trustworthiness of articles

nodes.label: Experience-driven calibration of trust
nodes.grounding_span: I will independently verify the source, such as its authors or if it was done at a university
